Best Tax Saving Investments Under 80C — 2026 Guide

Complete comparison of all Section 80C options in 2026. Find the right tax-saving investment based on your goals, risk appetite, and time horizon.

Updated: March 2026

Our Top Picks

Best Guaranteed Returns
#1 Pick
PPF (Public Provident Fund)
7.1% p.a. (guaranteed, tax-free)

The gold standard for safe, tax-free investment. Government-backed EEE status means your deposits, interest earned, and maturity amount are ALL tax-free. Best for anyone who prioritises capital safety.

  • Guaranteed 7.1% returns, zero risk
  • Complete EEE tax exemption
  • ₹500 to ₹1,50,000 annual investment
  • Partial withdrawal from Year 7
Calculate PPF Returns →
Best for High Returns
#2 Pick
ELSS Mutual Funds
12-18% p.a. (historical avg, not guaranteed)

Equity-linked savings with the shortest lock-in of just 3 years. Top funds like Mirae Asset and Axis Long Term have delivered 15-18% CAGR over 5 years. Best for investors comfortable with market risk.

  • Shortest lock-in among 80C options (3 years)
  • Highest return potential of all 80C investments
  • SIP starting from ₹500/month
  • Diversified equity portfolio with professional management
Explore ELSS Funds →
Best for Retirement
#3 Pick
NPS (National Pension System)
8-10% p.a. (historical avg)

Extra ₹50,000 deduction under Section 80CCD(1B) over and above 80C limit. Mix of equity, corporate bonds, and government securities. Best for long-term retirement planning with additional tax benefit.

  • Additional ₹50K deduction under 80CCD(1B)
  • Total tax saving up to ₹2 lakh/year
  • Mix of equity + debt for balanced growth
  • 60% corpus tax-free at retirement
Open NPS Account →

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Section 80C and how much can I save?

Section 80C of the Income Tax Act allows individuals to claim deductions up to ₹1,50,000 per financial year from their taxable income. This means if you are in the 30% tax bracket, you can save up to ₹46,800 in taxes (including cess). Investments like PPF, ELSS, NSC, NPS, tax-saving FD, and life insurance premium qualify under 80C.

Which is the best tax saving investment for 2026?

The best 80C investment depends on your goals: PPF for guaranteed tax-free returns with zero risk, ELSS for highest growth potential with 3-year lock-in, and NPS for retirement planning with extra ₹50K deduction. For most people, a combination of PPF + ELSS provides the best balance of safety and growth.

Can I invest in multiple 80C options?

Yes, you can invest in multiple 80C instruments. The total deduction across all 80C investments is capped at ₹1,50,000 per year. Many investors split their allocation — for example, ₹75K in PPF + ₹75K in ELSS, or ₹50K each in PPF, ELSS, and NPS.

Which 80C investment gives the highest returns?

ELSS mutual funds have historically delivered the highest returns among 80C investments at 12-18% CAGR over 5 years. However, these returns are market-linked and not guaranteed. For guaranteed returns, PPF offers 7.1% (fully tax-free) and NSC offers 7.7% (taxable interest).

What is the safest tax saving investment?

PPF, NSC, SSY, and EPF are the safest 80C investments as they are all government-backed with guaranteed returns. Among these, PPF offers the best combination of safety + tax efficiency due to its EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) status, meaning deposits, interest, and maturity are all tax-free.

Is NPS better than PPF for tax saving?

NPS offers an additional ₹50,000 deduction under Section 80CCD(1B) over and above the ₹1.5L limit of 80C, potentially saving ₹15,600 extra in taxes (30% bracket). However, NPS is locked till age 60 and partially taxable. PPF is more flexible with EEE status. For maximum tax saving, invest in both.

What about Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)?

SSY is an excellent option if you have a girl child under 10 years of age. It offers 8.2% p.a. (higher than PPF) with full EEE tax-free status. The 21-year lock-in is long, but partial withdrawal is allowed after the girl turns 18 for education expenses.

Should I invest in ULIP for tax saving?

ULIPs are generally not recommended solely for tax saving. They have high charges (mortality, fund management, administration) that eat into returns. ELSS offers similar equity exposure with lower costs and shorter lock-in. ULIPs may suit those who specifically want insurance + investment in one product.
